_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to use phrases when we talk to different people at school, thinking about who they are — like an adult or a friend.

_source.w: We are learning to use phrases when we talk to different people at school, thinking about who they are — like an adult or a friend.
y: We talk differently to different people — knowing this helps us communicate well in every situation.
Understanding relationships helps us choose the right words and tone.
This skill helps us get along with all the people in our school community.
eg: We practise greeting a teacher differently from greeting a friend.
We talk about how we speak to adults compared to how we speak to other children.
We role-play asking a teacher for help versus asking a classmate.
We notice that we might say 'please' and 'thank you' more formally with adults.
We practise introducing ourselves to a visitor at school.
We discuss why we might speak louder on the playground but softer in the library.
